text.title: quality-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for quality-management as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Process metric becomes a gaming target (Goodhart's Law): teams hit the coverage percentage by testing trivial code paths while leaving critical paths untested.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Rotate which metrics gate vs. which inform; a metric used as a gate for more than two cycles is at risk of being gamed. Supplement coverage percentage with mutation testing or branch coverage.

failure-mode.description: Agent writing "evidence" for a DoD item without a traceable artefact source.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fabricated evidence creates audit liability. Every pass assertion must point to an immutable artefact URL (CI run, report file, scan SARIF). Refuse to pass any criterion without a concrete source.

failure-mode.description: Defect logs sent to a third-party LLM without PII scrubbing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Defect logs routinely contain user IDs, email addresses, payment data, and health information. Scrub before sending to any external model.

failure-mode.description: Conflates Cost-of-Quality (prevention + appraisal + failure) with mere QA budget; teams under-invest in prevention because failure cost is invisible.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Separate the budget lines: prevention (linting, type checking, design review), appraisal (testing, scanning, audits), internal failure (rework, retests), external failure (production incidents, churn). Track CoQ trend to show ROI.

failure-mode.description: Definition of Done is a checkbox cargo cult unless tied to measurable acceptance criteria and runtime evidence (test coverage, security scan, perf baseline).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every DoD item must have a testable evidence source. If you cannot automate it or point to a concrete artefact, remove it from DoD and move it to "nice to have".

failure-mode.description: "Quality is everyone's job" → in practice no one owns it; missing named QA owner per workstream is a common failure.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Name a quality owner per workstream and codify in CODEOWNERS. Quality is everyone's job only if someone is explicitly responsible.

failure-mode.description: Static checklists rot; programs accumulate dozens of checklists that nobody updates.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Version quality-plan.yaml in git with branch protection. Checklist updates are PRs, not email updates.

failure-mode.description: LLMs grading "code quality" produce confident but inconsistent verdicts; deterministic linters and SAST beat narrative review for pass/fail gates.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use linters, type checkers, and SAST for pass/fail gates. Use LLMs for recommendations and patterns, but not for gate decisions.

failure-mode.description: UAT sign-off cannot substitute for production telemetry; defects escape UAT every release.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: UAT is a gate on requirements conformance. Production telemetry (error rate, latency p99, SLOs) is a separate gate. Both are mandatory.

failure-mode.description: Agents reading test reports skip the parts they cannot interpret (binary blobs, large logs); always pre-process to structured JSON before prompt.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Binary output is agent-unfriendly. Transform test reports to JSON before feeding to an agent.

failure-mode.description: Defect triage agents over-merge duplicates because of surface similarity; require >0.85 embedding cosine + matching environment before auto-link.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Over-merging duplicates erases signal from the bug tracker. Require high confidence (multiple signals) before suggesting a duplicate.

failure-mode.description: Severity inflation: agents rate everything S2/S3 to be "safe"; force a calibration set with examples in the prompt.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A severity rubric with concrete examples (S1: production down, customer impact; S2: feature broken, workaround exists; S3: cosmetic) is the only anchor.

failure-mode.description: Agents writing defect reports invent reproduction steps; ground on actual logs/traces and refuse to fabricate steps.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Invented repro steps cannot be executed. Quality gates must reject fabricated evidence.

failure-mode.description: Privacy: defect logs frequently leak PII (user IDs, emails, payment data); scrub before sending to a third-party model.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: PII in defect logs is a data breach risk and audit liability. Scrub before feeding to any external model.

failure-mode.description: Audit-prep agents producing "evidence" without traceable source create regulatory liability; every evidence claim must point to an immutable artifact.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fabricated audit evidence is worse than no evidence. Every claim must have a traceable source (CI run, report file, scan SARIF with timestamp).

failure-mode.description: Auto-closing duplicates without human review erases information; agent recommends, human merges.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Auto-merge of duplicates can hide important signal (different root cause, different severity, different environment). Require human review.

failure-mode.description: Coverage hallucination: agents claim coverage that exceeds the actual report; always trust the file, not the LLM summary.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents invent metrics. Coverage comes from the report file, not from the LLM paraphrase. Cross-check every metric against the source file.
